commit.message:
docs: restructure CLAUDE.md and fix worktree path resolution in setup script (#26)

* fix(automation): use git-common-dir for worktree-to-main-project resolution

`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` in a worktree returns the worktree root,
not the main project root. Using `dirname $(git rev-parse --path-format=absolute
--git-common-dir)` correctly resolves to the main project directory from any
worktree path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: restructure CLAUDE.md - extract verbose reference sections

Reduces CLAUDE.md from ~1112 lines to 452 lines (~59% reduction) by moving
14 detailed reference sections into docs/CLAUDE-REFERENCE.md. Each section
replaced with a 1-3 line summary and a direct anchor link. All operational
context, commands, and behavioral instructions remain inline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: IPVLAN overlay reconciliation + dual-fabric auto-detect (#127-#130)

#127: Reconcile IPVLAN addresses on reapply — add missing, remove stale
addresses instead of skipping when overlay already exists on correct parent.

#128: Clean up stale fab0/fab1 overlays during config transitions —
inline cleanup in applyConfig removes interfaces not in active set.

#129: Probe ARP/NDP on overlay interface (fab0/fab1), not parent —
neighbor learning uses overlay ifindex, FabricFwdInfo uses parent ifindex
for XDP redirect. monitorFabricState watches overlay link state too.

#130: Auto-detect both FabricInterface and Fabric1Interface from fab0/fab1
syntax. Derive Fabric1PeerAddress from /30 or /31 point-to-point CIDR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace broken SOAP GetMagazalar with REST API for store locator

GetMagazalar does not exist in Ticimax CustomServis WSDL, causing
silent failures. Now uses sonaxshop.com.tr/api/Store/GetStoriesLite
REST endpoint which returns all 21 stores correctly. Also improved
Turkish character normalization for better city search.

commit.message:
fix(desktop,ingest): extract QR URL from DOM and regenerate clean QR PNG

Instead of screenshotting Signal Desktop's QR (which gets destroyed by
threshold artifacts and Signal's JPEG recompression), extract the sgnl://
linking URL from the DOM via DevTools and regenerate a clean QR code
using the qrcode library. Falls back to screenshot if DOM extraction fails.

commit.message:
feat(agent): sistema de cola para CSF/Opinión de todos los clientes

- loadRfcsFromFiel(): detecta todos los RFCs con FIEL completa en fiel/
- runScraperForRfc(rfc): ejecuta scraper_sat.js y detecta éxito por stdout
- processQueue(): procesa la cola secuencialmente (un browser a la vez)
  - Cada RFC tiene hasta 3 intentos externos (outer attempts)
  - Si falla → al final de la cola; si agota 3 intentos → se descarta
  - Los 3 intentos internos de CSF y 32-D ya estaban en scraper_sat.js
- POST /enqueue-all: carga todos los RFCs e inicia la cola
- GET  /queue-status: devuelve estado actual (running, pending, queue)

Fix SIN/GIN/CIN ERR caused by special chars (e.g. parentheses) in names

break the protocol; parentheses and other special chars cause ERR responses.

fix: fabric health + gRPC dual-address failover (#121, #122, #124, #125)

#121: Clear stale fabric_fwd entries when fabric path dies — write
zeroed FabricFwdInfo{Ifindex:0} so dataplane falls back to other fabric.

#122: Check oper-state before programming fabric_fwd — reject non-UP
interfaces and clear their entries immediately.

#124: Add netlink-driven refresh for fabric link/neighbor changes via
LinkSubscribe + NeighSubscribe. 30s ticker kept as safety net.

#125: Make peer gRPC/monitor path dual-address-aware — listen on both
fabric IPs, dial with fab0→fab1 fallback, refactor CLI peer dialing
to use shared dialPeer() helper.

fix(desktop): resize then threshold QR for cleaner module edges

Upscale with bilinear resize before thresholding so anti-aliased edges
are smooth before binarization, avoiding single-pixel noise artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: per-fabric session sync connections, prevent flapping (#123)

Replace single mutable s.conn with per-fabric conn0/conn1 slots.
fab0 is always preferred; fab1 is used only when fab0 is down.

Key changes:
- Track connections per-fabric (conn0 for fab0, conn1 for fab1)
- activeConnLocked() picks conn0 if available, else conn1
- handleNewConnection() sets the right slot, only triggers BulkSync
  on first connection after total disconnect (not when adding
  redundant fabric path)
- Per-fabric fabricConnectLoop() replaces single rotating connectLoop
- handleDisconnect() identifies which fabric to clear by pointer
  equality, only marks fully disconnected when both fabrics are down
- writeMu still serializes ALL writes (unchanged, critical for sync)
- writeFull() still loops until all bytes sent (unchanged)
- ActiveFabric() reports which fabric carries traffic (0, 1, or -1)
- SyncStatsSnapshot.ActiveFabric for status display

fix: restore seed token fallback when CoinGecko is unavailable

fetchMarketContext() was throwing MarketDataUnavailableError when
CoinGecko returned empty (e.g. 429 rate limit), which caused all
agent posting to halt. Restore the fallback to seedTokens so agents
can continue posting with static price data when the API is down.

feat(patterns): add mandatory audit trail for dismissed findings to pr-codex-bot (#357)

* feat(patterns): add mandatory audit trail for dismissed findings to pr-codex-bot

* feat(pr-codex-bot,cli-sub-agent): harden PR audit trail with fail-closed parsing and explicit comment posting

Add Step 8a / workflow step 19 that explicitly posts dismissed-finding
audit trail comments via `gh pr comment --body-file` with fail-closed
parsing: duplicate VERDICT markers, missing PR_COMMENT_START/END pairs,
and absent required sections all abort instead of silently proceeding.
CONFIRMED verdicts reroute to the fix step without posting a dismissal
comment.

Clarify audit trail scope: PR-page findings only (local pre-PR findings
must be fixed before PR creation). Add 4 integration tests covering
dismissed, confirmed, malformed, and duplicate-verdict paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(desktop): upscale QR to 800x800 8-bit grayscale so it survives Signal's JPEG recompression

The 300x300 1-bit QR screenshots were unscannable after Signal re-compressed
them as JPEG when sending as attachments. Now we upscale with nearest-neighbor
sampling to 800x800 and output as 8-bit grayscale PNG, preserving sharp module
edges even after lossy recompression. Also bumped blank-detection threshold
from 2000 to 5000 bytes to match the larger output.

Community detection:
- scripts/compute_communities.py: Louvain with fixed seed, community 0=largest

Frontend (StorylineGraph):
- Physics: warmupTicks=300, cooldownTicks=0, d3AlphaDecay=0.05 (frozen layout)
- Ego-network UX: click node → useEgoNetwork → dim non-neighbors (alpha 0.08),

- GraphNode interface + graphData useMemo now include community_id

commit.message:
fix: mark DPDK fabric redirect as unsupported (#126)

The DPDK dataplane programs fabric port IDs for inbound zone-encoded
MAC detection but has no equivalent of the BPF try_fabric_redirect()
and try_fabric_redirect_with_zone() helpers. Add slog.Warn calls in
UpdateFabricFwd/UpdateFabricFwd1 and clarify the limitation in
dpdk_worker/zone.c comments.

Closes #126

commit.message:
Fix factory defaults persisting; fix SIN failing silently on bad fields

Alabama/factory systems fix:
  DSY cannot delete protected factory systems on its own.
  New approach: send CLR first (resets scanner AND puts factory defaults
  back into the normal linked list with no protect lock), then immediately
  walk the linked list with DSY to delete every system including the
  factory ones. CLR + DSY together produces a truly blank scanner.

SIN 'System 1' name fix:
  Any format error causes SIN to abort silently and keep the scanner's
  default system name. Added field sanitisation before building the
  SIN command:
  - name: strip whitespace, truncate to 16 chars
  - hold_time: clamp to 0-255 integer, default 2
  - delay_time: snap to nearest valid value (-10,-5,-2,0,1,2,5,10,30)
  - quick_key: default to '.' if empty
  SIN response is now logged so a failure is visible in the upload log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
